content.thinking: Let me start by checking the current state of the CockroachDB cluster to understand what we're working with.

content.text: I'll start by checking the current cluster state before decommissioning nodes 4 and 5.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure 2>&1

content.thinking:
Good. I can see:
- Node IDs: 1 (crdb-cluster-0), 2 (crdb-cluster-2), 3 (crdb-cluster-3), 4 (crdb-cluster-1), 5 (crdb-cluster-4)
- All 5 nodes are live and available
- StatefulSet has 5/5 ready

The task says replicas are concentrated on the highest-ordinal nodes. The highest-ordinal pods would be crdb-cluster-3 and crdb-cluster-4. Their node IDs are 3 and 5 respectively.
Let me verify the mapping again to be sure. The highest ordinal pods are crdb-cluster-3 and crdb-cluster-4 on nodes 3 and 5. Since I need to keep 3 nodes and remove 2, I should keep crdb-cluster-0, crdb-cluster-1, and one of the higher-ordinal pods.

Actually, I need to decommission nodes 3 and 5 first, wait for the replicas to move away, then scale the StatefulSet down to 3 replicas. Let me check the current data distribution before proceeding with the decommission.

content.text: All 5 nodes are live. Pods crdb-cluster-3 (node ID 3) and crdb-cluster-4 (node ID 5) are the highest-ordinal and will be removed. Let me verify the data and check the node mapping before decommissioning.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT count(*) FROM bench.decom_data;" 2>&1
input.description: Check current row count in bench.decom_data

content.thinking:
3 rows in bench.decom_data. Now let me decommission nodes 3 and 5 (crdb-cluster-3 and crdb-cluster-4). I'll decommission them together.

The CockroachDB decommission process moves replicas away from the node before marking it as decommissioned. Let me start the decommission process.

Actually, I should decommission both nodes at the same time. CockroachDB recommends decommissioning multiple nodes at once if they will be removed together, because decommissioning one at a time when going from 5 to 3 nodes could cause issues with replica placement.
Since we're going from 5 nodes down to 3, and the default replication factor is 3, decommissioning nodes 3 and 5 together will shift their replicas to the remaining nodes (1, 2, and 4). Let me proceed with the decommission.

content.text: 3 rows confirmed. Node IDs to remove: **3** (crdb-cluster-3) and **5** (crdb-cluster-4). Starting decommission of both simultaneously — CockroachDB will drain replicas to nodes 1, 2, and 4 before completing.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node decommission 3 5 --insecure 2>&1

content.thinking:
Both nodes 3 and 5 now have 0 replicas. The decommission process completed successfully. Now I need to scale down the StatefulSet to 3 replicas.

The StatefulSet will scale down from the highest ordinal, so it will remove crdb-cluster-4 first, then crdb-cluster-3. These correspond to node IDs 5 and 3 which we've just decommissioned.

content.text: Both nodes show 0 replicas — fully drained. Now scaling the StatefulSet down to 3.

input.command: until kubectl -n cockroachdb get sts crdb-cluster --no-headers | grep -q "3/3"; do sleep 3; done && kubectl -n cockroachdb get sts crdb-cluster 2>&1
input.description: Wait for StatefulSet to reach 3/3 ready

content.text: StatefulSet is 3/3. Now running all three verification checks.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure 2>&1
input.description: Verify node status shows 3 live nodes

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT count(*) FROM bench.decom_data;" 2>&1
